channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: At UN climate talks, nations blast draft of vague deal on climate cash for developing countries
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — Countries of the world took turns rejecting a new but vague draft text released early Thursday which attempts to form the spine of any deal reached at United Nations climate talks on money for developing countries to transition to clean energy and adapt to climate change.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, SIBI ARASU and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EX-UK Deputy Prime Minister John Prescott is hailed as a working-class hero after his death at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British politician John Prescott, a pugnacious and personable former merchant seaman who rose to the post of deputy prime minister in Tony Blair's Labour government, has died at age 86. Prescott’s family announced his death on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine says Russia launched an intercontinental missile in an attack for the first time in the war
item.description: Ukraine says Russia has launched an intercontinental ballistic missile overnight targeting Dnipro city in the central-east of the country, which, if confirmed, would be the first time Moscow has used such a missile in the war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Volcano on Iceland's Reykjanes Peninsula erupts for the 7th time in a year
item.description: A volcano on the Reykjanes Peninsula in southwestern Iceland is spewing lava from a fissure in its seventh eruption since December.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: About 1,500 migrants form a new caravan in Mexico. Here's what it means
item.description: TAPACHULA, Mexico (AP) — About 1,500 migrants formed a new caravan Wednesday in southern Mexico, hoping to walk or catch rides to the U.S. border. The migrants are mainly from Central and South America.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia's parliament considers legislation banning social media for under 16s
item.description: Legislation for the world's first legal ban on children under 16 using social media has been introduced in Australia's Parliament.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding a cease-fire in Gaza because there's no link to a hostage release
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Wednesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the war in Gaza because it was not linked to an immediate release of hostages taken captive by Hamas militants in Israel in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy answers whether he's willing to cede Crimea, other territory in peace deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy tells Fox News that his country "cannot legally acknowledge any occupied territory of Ukraine as Russian.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctors Without Borders suspends operations in Haiti
item.description: Doctors Without Borders has suspended its activities in Port-au-Prince following a series of threats by Haiti's police forces, the organization announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Economists expect Trump to place huge tariffs on Chinese imports: Survey
item.description: Economists are expecting President-elect Trump to place tariffs of about 40 percent on Chinese imports, according to a survey from Reuters. In the survey of economists, 45 percent said the “level of tariffs imposed by Trump on Chinese goods” will be between 31 percent and 45 percent.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Russian lawmakers endorse bill to ban adoptions by gender-transition countries
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia’s upper house of parliament on Wednesday endorsed a bill banning adoption of Russian children by citizens of countries where gender transitioning is legal. The Federation Council also approved bills that outlaw the spread of material that encourages people not to have children.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hong Kong ex-publisher Jimmy Lai testifies he didn't ask Pence, Pompeo to take action against city
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Former Hong Kong publisher Jimmy Lai denied in his landmark national security trial on Wednesday he had asked then U.S.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Embassy in Kyiv closed as 'potential significant air attack' looms
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Kyiv, Ukraine, has released a warning stating that they have "specific information of a potential significant air attack" taking place on Wednesday, Nov. 20.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN accused of downplaying Hamas terrorists' use of Gaza hospitals as new report ignores important details
item.description: IDF reported encountering terrorists in hospitals but Hamas' health ministry refuses to admit the group's presence there. Critics say the U.N. and others are too quick to parrot Hamas talking points.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The mausoleum holding Congo independence hero Lumumba's gold-capped tooth is vandalized
item.description: KINSHASA, Congo (AP) — Congo’s government says the mausoleum of assassinated independence leader Patrice Lumumba in the capital, Kinshasa, has been vandalized. It's not immediately clear whether Lumumba’s remains — a single gold-capped tooth — were damaged or stolen.
item.dc:creator: JEAN-YVES KAMALE and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia's plan to ban children from social media proves popular and problematic
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — How do you remove children from the harms of social media? Politically the answer appears simple in Australia, but practically the solution could be far more difficult.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Leader of Georgia's separatist region of Abkhazia resigns, ending unrest
item.description: Following days of unrest in which the opposition seized government buildings, the leader of Georgia's separatist region of Abkhazia has resigned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1,000 days of war in Ukraine as Zelenskyy doubles down on aerial options with ATACMS, drones and missiles
item.description: Ukraine marks 1,000 days of war with Russia on Tuesday as President Zelenskyy eyes plan to develop thousands of long-range drones and military targets inside Russia using US-supplied ATACMS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US allies accuse Russia of 'escalating hybrid activities' against NATO, EU nations after data cables severed
item.description: U.S. allies accuse Russia of "escalating hybrid activities" against NATO and EU countries after two data cables were severed in the Baltic Sea this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More Ukrainians want talks to end war with Russia: Gallup
item.description: More than half of Ukrainians want talks to end the war with Russia, according to Gallup polls released Tuesday, the war's 1,000th day.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine fires US-made ATACMS at Russia for first time
item.description: Ukraine fired U.S.-made long-range missiles inside of Russia on Tuesday for the first time since a restriction was lifted by Washington, according to Russian officials.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Putin lowers threshold for Russian nuclear weapons use after Biden's Ukraine missile decision
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Tuesday lowered the threshold for nuclear weapons use, just two days after the U.S. authorized Ukraine to use long-range missiles inside of Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Biden's Ukraine missile move will have 'some impact': Retired major
item.description: Lyons questioned the timing of the decision, saying, “I’m not sure why we didn’t allow this two or three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, The Hill

item.title: Putin signs revised doctrine lowering threshold for nuclear response if Russia is attacked
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a revised nuclear doctrine stating that any attack on Russia supported by a country with nuclear power could potentially trigger a nuclear response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Bolton: Moscow, Beijing should feel ‘a lot of relief’ if Gabbard is confirmed as DNI
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton said that Russia and China should feel “a lot of relief” if former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) is confirmed as President-elect Trump’s choice for director of national intelligence. “She’s not capable of doing it,” Bolton said Sunday on CNN.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu confirms Israel strike against Iran hit nuke program during October retaliatory strikes
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed that part of Iran's nuclear program was degraded after IDF strikes on military facilities in and around Tehran in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Australian zoo asks citizens to catch deadly spiders
item.description: Despite the zany ask, there is a valiant reason behind the call from The Australian Reptile Park in Sydney, New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Wreckage of World War II warship found 80 years later
item.description: The USS Edsall, a World War II American warship, was bombed and sank three months after the attack on Pearl Harbor.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: Severe flight turbulence on Florida-bound flight sends person airborne, requires plane inspection
item.description: A Miami-bound flight turned frightening after the plane experienced severe turbulence, causing a diversion and requiring inspection of the plane.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US health officials report 1st case of new form of mpox in a traveler
item.description: Health officials said Saturday they have confirmed the first U.S. case of a new form of mpox that was first seen in eastern Congo.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: T-Mobile hacked in massive Chinese cyber-espionage: Wall St. Journal
item.description: It was part of a campaign to spy on the phones of high-value intelligence targets, the Journal reports.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Montana Tucker's Oct. 7 film showcases young victims' strength
item.description: Montana Tucker's film, "The Children of October 7," documents what some of the attacks' youngest victims went through.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Alex Soros, Jared Kushner reportedly obsessed with Albania
item.description: Alex Soros, whose father is Hungarian, reportedly travels to the capital Tirana so often he has called it “my second home.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Moo Deng, Thailand's baby pygmy hippo, gets official song in 4 languages
item.description: In case you can’t get enough of the little pygmy hippo Moo Deng from Thailand, there's now an official song featuring the internet's favorite baby animal — released in four languages for her global fans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
